TextENGNew: I arrived at the Okinomiya Library thirty minutes ahead of schedule.
The weather was beautiful, just as I'd hoped. ...Well, once we were inside it wouldn't matter whether it was sunny or rainy, but it made a difference to the mood.
Under the clear sky, the early summer sun felt pleasant on my skin. I breathed in the crisp, clean air until my lungs were full, parked my bicycle at the bike rack, and headed for the main entrance.
I'd gotten here earlier than expected. Mion surely hadn't arrived yet. ...With that thought, I walked past the entrance, figuring I'd go buy a canned drink.
Just then.
I nearly walked right past her—she was so inconspicuous I almost missed her—but there was Mion, standing in the corner by the main gate.
``G-good morning...``
``Oh... hey.@k@vS14/01/210101173. You're here awfully early.``
``Ah, ahaha...@k@vS14/03/210300545. I-I got the time mixed up, is all.``
Mion gave an awkward, embarrassed laugh as she said that. ...Got the time mixed up, she says—then what exactly is that watch on her wrist for? I was tempted to point that out.
Mion was dressed in her usual outfit, her hair done up in its usual style.
——I'd asked Rena to subtly suggest that Mion come in everyday clothes, and it seemed like the message got through.
Well, I was a bit curious to see Mion all dressed up... but I figured she'd only get more flustered if she tried too hard, so I'd save that for next time.
That said, despite her ordinary outfit, she'd applied a delicate touch of rouge to her lips—a soft pink that made her look subtly cuter than usual.
If I'm not mistaken, that lipstick was from the brand that just came out recently. Thanks to it, I'd been subjected to a nightmare during a previous club activity...
——No. Let's not go there.
``...You seem kinda girly today, Mion.``
``N-no way!@k@vS14/03/210300547. I-I'm the same as always!``
``Ah, ahahahaha~``
...I was expecting a comeback along the lines of 'I'm always a girl, you know!' But she didn't. She must be really wound up today... Mion, that is.
``Did you bring everything?@k@vS14/01/210101176. Your reference books and workbooks.``
``O-of course!@k@vS14/03/210300550. There's no way I'd forget~``
Deep down, I breathed a sigh of relief.
...It seemed the worst-case scenario had been averted, at least.
Just yesterday she'd asked me, 'K-Kei-chan, what do you want to eat tomorrow?' of all things.
She'd been in such a state of total panic that I'd had to remind her of the basics—'What exactly are we going there to do?'
...Though I still couldn't rule out the possibility that she'd accidentally grabbed a video game strategy guide instead.
``Well then, shall we?``
``Y-yeah!``
Mion gave a big nod, her face flushed, then fell into step beside me with stiff, awkward movements.
...Hey, Mion—your arms and legs are moving in sync.

The moment we stepped through the library's automatic doors, we were enveloped in the crisp chill of carefully controlled air conditioning. It was refreshing, but since I hadn't fully warmed up from the midday heat yet, it felt a bit too cold.

Mion and I made our way to the study room and looked for seats that were far from the air conditioning vents. Being too cold makes it easy to feel unwell, and it saps your concentration over long stretches. A teacher at my old cram school had taught me that.
``Ah, those seats over there are open.``
I spotted a good spot in the corner of the room. It was a four-person table by the window, not in direct sunlight.
Although it was technically a study room, it wasn't nearly as crowded as the ones back in the city. So Mion and I decided to sit across from each other at the table.
*THUNK*
``A-are you okay...?``
``T-t-totally fine!@k@vS14/03/210300554. Completely fine!@k@vS14/03/210300555. Ah, ahahaha...``
It looked like Mion had banged her knee against the table as she tried to sit down. She was still moving pretty stiffly...
Even with four chairs, the table was surprisingly small. If we each spread out a reference book, notebook, and dictionary, the whole surface would be covered.
``This table's pretty small, huh.``
``Y-yeah.``
``...Should we put one dictionary away and share?``
``S-sure... whoa?!``
The moment our eyes met, Mion flinched and jerked backward.

At a table this small, sitting face to face naturally meant our faces were quite close together. Mion apparently hadn't prepared herself mentally for that—she kept fidgeting and acting oddly restless.
``R-r-r-right.@k@vS14/03/210300559. It is... really... small, so sharing... one dictionary... might be best...``
Her words were barely coherent. Was this atmosphere getting more and more awkward by the minute...?
*roll*
As Mion went to put the dictionary away, she accidentally knocked the eraser beside it off the table. She scrambled to pick it up from the floor, but—
*thud*
As she straightened up, her shoulder caught on the table leg, sending her mechanical pencil rolling off the opposite side. She hastily crouched down again to retrieve it, but—
*BONK*
``Ow ow ow?!``
She smacked her head on the corner of the table coming back up, and the impact sent her reference book sliding off the other edge.
``Oww... eeeep~...``
``Hey, Mion...?``
I picked up the reference book and handed it back to Mion, who was clutching the back of her head with tears in her eyes. Then I spoke up, half exasperated.
``First things first... calm down.@k@vS14/01/210101184. Take a deep breath.``
``Oh... I-I'm sor—?!``
Before Mion could finish apologizing, I took her hand firmly and pulled her close. When she looked up in surprise, I gave her a warm, reassuring smile.
``...Kei-chan?``
``You haven't done anything wrong, so there's no need to apologize.@k@vS14/01/210101186. ...We're friends, aren't we?``

``I can tell you're nervous and your head's a mess, but try to relax.@k@vS14/01/210101188. I'm Keiichi Maebara, and you're Mion Sonozaki.@k@vS14/01/210101189. That doesn't change whether anyone else is around or not, right?``
``Think back to that night when I called and asked you to help Rena.``
When Rena was in despair over her father's situation... the very first person who came to mind was Mion. Because I was certain she could handle it.
If it had been just Rika-chan and me trying to comfort Rena as she cried, our words probably would have never reached her.
No matter how modestly she downplayed it, I knew the truth. Rena's rapid recovery was entirely thanks to Mion.
changeReason: Narrative perspective shifts from third person to first person.

TextENGNew: ``...You're right...``

At last, Mion mopped the sweat from her brow with her handkerchief and let out a long, exhausted sigh. Then, after whispering something to herself under her breath a few times, she lifted her face and smiled.
``Haah~, I'm totally drained.@k@vS14/03/210300573. Now that I think about it, you and I have never really gone out alone together, have we?``
``Even though we have such a blast with everyone every single day~?@k@vS14/03/210300575. So I guess I just got weirdly worked up about it~, ahahahaha!``
``Even so, you were way too worked up...@k@vS14/01/210101192. But yeah, I guess you've got a point.``
I'd gone out with just Rena plenty of times—to the junkyard and all—but I naturally had the good sense not to mention that.

``I've just been overthinking everything~@k@vS14/03/210300577. You know how the only guys I ever talk to are either younger boys or old men, right?@k@vS14/03/210300578. So I wasn't sure how to act when it's just the two of us face to face!``
``Face to face, what does that even mean...@k@vS14/01/210101194. And for the record, I'm technically younger than you too.``
``Well, yeah... that's true, but...@k@vS14/03/210300580. How do I put it—once I started being conscious of it, things just felt different compared to being around Rena and the others.``
``...Conscious?``
...Huh—was it just me, or were Mion's eyebrows angled like the hands of a clock at ten to two...?
``...Haah, you're right.@k@vS14/03/210300583. This is just you we're talking about, so there's no reason to act any different from usual~``
With a weary expression, Mion nodded to herself as if she'd reached some private conclusion. ...I had no idea what she meant, but at least she seemed to be back to normal. Good enough.
``All right!@k@vS14/01/210101197. Let's get to studying~!``
``Okay~!``
After all that fuss, Mion and I finally buckled down and started studying.
Thirty minutes in.
``Hey, Kei-chan.@k@vS14/03/210300586. Do you think this translation is right...?``
``Which one?@k@vS14/01/210101199. Let me see...``
Then again, Mion was a grade above me—could I even help with her problems...?
With that thought, I leaned over to look at the question she was pointing to.
Wait... this? Her answer to this problem is really... this?!
``Wh-what's wrong, Kei-chan? Is it too hard for you?``
I was struck dumb when I saw the question Mion was struggling with.
This was absolutely, without question, the most elementary of elementary entrance exam material. We're talking 'literal first step' level...!
How elementary, you ask? ...Well, for the sake of the Sonozaki family heiress's honor, let's just say anyone who'd done even minimal exam prep would be enraged.
``...Just to be sure, let me ask.@k@vS14/01/210101202. In English grammar, 'S, V, O, C'—what do those stand for?``
``Umm... Brandy?``
``That's V.S.O.P!!``
For the record, the correct answer is 'Subject, Verb, Object, Complement.' And V.S.O.P. is a classification for brandy aging. Naturally, the two have absolutely no connection. None whatsoever.
...Hold on—isn't this covered in compulsory education?
``Mion...@k@vS14/01/210101205. Let me take a look at your notebook.``
``Huh? Oh—``
A terrible suspicion forming in my mind, I grabbed Mion's notebook and checked the other sections.
Sure enough... no, it was even worse than I'd imagined. Wrong answers everywhere. Wrong, wrong, wrong, and wrong again... And some of the English wasn't even English—it was romaji?!
T-to think it was this bad... Mion... truly terrifying...
``What?``
``This is... really something...``
``Huh? ...Really something... good?``
``More like really something reckless!@k@vS14/01/210101210. ...What on earth have you been studying all this time?!``
``W-well~, this old man's motto has always been practical experience over book learning...@k@vS14/03/210300593. Aha, ahahahaha...``
She'd told me that before. ...Still, this was bad. Catastrophically bad.
``I'm amazed you've made it this far in school...``
``Hey! Don't say things that'll kill my motivation, Kei-chan!@k@vS14/03/210300595. Not to someone who's about to take entrance exams!``
...From the perspective of anyone currently suffering through exam hell in the city, I was confident that was an exceptionally gentle assessment...

``Listen here~!@k@vS14/03/210300597. Our school is the kind of place where you can pass just by naming the capitals of different countries!@k@vS14/03/210300598. Don't hold me to your standards, Kei-chan!``
``What delinquent manga did you get that from?!@k@vS14/01/210101213. ...By the way, what's the capital of Brazil?``
``Geneva.``
And she got it wrong! ...Haah, I'm seriously worried about her future here...?
``...Anyway, the goal is to bring you up to entrance exam level, right?``
``Yes!@k@vS14/03/210300601. I'm fired up and ready to give it my all~!``
``You really... and I mean really, really... need to try hard here...``
``Ahahahaha, leave it to me!@k@vS14/03/210300603. I may not look it, but I'm great under pressure! It'll be fine!``
...Fine? She says it'll be fine?
``There is no universe in which this is fine, you blockhead!@k@vS14/01/210101217. If you could pass at this level, the number of students who pass would exceed the number who apply!``
``And what's this about being 'fired up'?!@k@vS14/01/210101219. You're making a catastrophic mistake if you think sheer willpower can solve everything!!``
``Even if Rika-chan says fate is the crystallization of human will, if you can survive entrance exams at your current ability, then people can fly through the sky on the back of an eel!!``
...Those words surged to the tip of my tongue, but I wrestled them back down with every last shred of conscience and reason I possessed.
If I said that... she'd cry. Even Mion would definitely cry. Absolutely, without question.
It would indeed be cruel to shatter the enthusiasm Mion had finally mustered with a cold dose of reality.
changeReason: New translation is entirely different content from the original, indicating a misaligned or completely different passage.

TextENGNew: And yet, however cruel the truth might be to share... why did I get the feeling that hiding it would be even crueler...?
Until now, I'd had no idea just how dire Mion's academic situation was.

Since she'd announced her intention to study so suddenly, I hadn't expected much... but the reality exceeded my worst imagination by such a margin that I almost regretted casually offering to tutor her.
``Even so...``
Now I was curious about something else entirely.
changeReason: New translation is entirely different content from the original, indicating a misaligned or completely different passage.

TextENGNew: Her academic record made it obvious that Mion had never seriously studied before. But in truth, as the heir to the Sonozaki family, academic prowess might not matter much.
changeReason: Narrative perspective shifts from first person to third person.

TextENGNew: So why had Mion suddenly decided to study for entrance exams?
``...Hey, Mion?``
``I-it's fine, I'll work really hard!@k@vS14/03/210300606. I know it looks bad right now, but I'll catch up in no time!@k@vS14/03/210300607. I mean it!``
``No, that's not what I'm asking...@k@vS14/01/210101224. What made you decide to start studying for entrance exams all of a sudden?``
Mion's face turned visibly red. ...Oh come on, not again.
All I'd asked was her motivation for studying—hardly the kind of question that warranted blushing...?
``Wh-what is it...?@k@vS14/01/210101226. Was that a weird thing to ask?``
It was a simple question about why she'd started studying... yet Mion kept fumbling over her words, struggling to get them out.
...Something was clearly off. And if she came back with a safe, noncommittal answer like 'No particular reason,' I'd be even less convinced.
``Well... if you don't want to say, you don't have to.@k@vS14/01/210101229. But as a friend, I'd like to know your reason.``
``...Th-then... maybe I'll... tell you...``
``Sure, go ahead, let's hear it~``
``I...@k@vS14/03/210300614. I was thinking it'd be nice to go to the same school! ...You know?``
``The same school......... with who?``
``...Obviously,@k@vS14/03/210300616. ...with you, Kei-chan.``
She started studying for entrance exams... because she wanted to go to... the same school as me?!
The answer was so far outside my expectations that I was struck speechless.
W-what was that explosion of devastatingly adorable moe energy in her answer?! Coming from the usual Mion, I'd never have imagined— N-no, going any further would be too much.
I-if she did that deliberately, she completely got the better of me... but if by some chance she was... actually serious... th-then, um...!
``E-e-even if you say 'the same school,' you're a year ahead of me, aren't you?!``
``R-right, so that's why!@k@vS14/03/210300618. ...I figured if I spent about a year studying, I could catch up...``
From what I'd just seen, catching up in a year seemed... optimistic. But no, never mind that.
Oh no, total self-destruction...! I could feel my own face heating up.
Of course I'm going to get flustered too if she says something that direct!
Calm down... just calm down, me... In... out...!
``...W-well... about that, you know...@k@vS14/01/210101234. I-I think it might be... fun, maybe? If we went to the same school, Mion?``
``...It might?``
``N-no, I'm sure it would be! Definitely! Yeah!!``
My voice cracked as I frantically strung words together. ...Come on, I told myself to calm down.
Besides... even if she wanted to aim for the same school, there was no reason she'd need to work this hard studying for exams just for that...

``At the rate you're going, Kei-chan... you'll end up at a university in Tokyo, right?@k@vS14/03/210300621. And if that happens... there's absolutely no way I could follow with my current grades. So...``
``I... I see... so@k@vS14/01/210101237. Mion, ...you're saying...?``
``That's right... I want to go to Tokyo too...``
...Yet another thing I hadn't expected. I never imagined Mion was considering leaving this village...
``...B-but what about the Sonozaki family?``
``I'll convince them to at least let me go for college.@k@vS14/03/210300624. I'm sure it'll... cause quite a stir within the family, though.``
...Yeah, no doubt. Even temporarily, the next head of the Sonozaki family leaving Hinamizawa would be a major issue.
``But Mion... were you always thinking about going to Tokyo?``

``Not at all, until very recently.@k@vS14/03/210300626. ...But once I started thinking about going to the same school as you, it was the first time I ever imagined a version of myself separate from the Sonozaki family.``
Mion smiled as she said that.

``It's true—I'm Hinamizawa's representative, the next head of the Sonozaki family.@k@vS14/03/210300628. So I was supposed to be born here, raised here, married here... have children here, and never leave until I died.``

``That's what I was taught since I was little. This village was everything I had. Finding fun things to do every day, making a ruckus with everyone, living happily...@k@vS14/03/210300630. I never really thought beyond that.``
``But, well, I gave it some thought.@k@vS14/03/210300632. Our village has been changing steadily.``
``Aside from the families who've lived here since ancient times, we're welcoming new ones like yours, Kei-chan. You can't just cling to the 'good old days' anymore.``
``...So I want to broaden my horizons.@k@vS14/03/210300635. Of course I meant it when I said I want to be with you, Kei-chan, but I also think this is something truly important for my own future.``
Then, tilting her head slightly, she asked me something.
``Hey, Kei-chan——``
``After college, do you think...@k@vS14/03/210300638. you'll stay in Tokyo...?``
``Hmm... I haven't thought that far ahead yet.@k@vS14/01/210101243. But I've been thinking it might not be a bad idea.``
I was actually surprised at how naturally those words came out. Life in Tokyo and the city was something I'd wanted to forget, to deny, ever since coming to Hinamizawa.
Even so.
The real reason I'd started thinking this way was because of all the conversations I'd had with the adults at that engagement party debacle—especially with Mion's relatives.
The Sonozaki family's reach extended into an extraordinary range of fields.
Some were politicians, others lawyers; there were restaurant chain owners and brand-name manufacturers too.
...And what they all had in common, despite being active both inside and outside the village, was a deep love for Hinamizawa.
Every one of them was giving their all to make this village a better place. They cultivated connections, knowledge, and experience to that end, then wielded those weapons with everything they had.

...In fact, the dam opposition movement from years past would have been dismissed as a mere local protest if these people hadn't worked through legitimate channels with effective procedures aimed at those in power.
In other words, it was no exaggeration to call them the greatest contributors to protecting this village.
...To fight, you need a weapon. To protect, you need strength.
As responsibility grows, so do obligations—and the need to confront the challenges and walls that stand in your way.

When faced with that kind of responsibility, what should you do? ...Once I became aware of that question, I began to feel that even my day-to-day studying was far more than just a puzzle-solving game.
There was a time when I studied without finding any meaning in it.
I used to be happy just getting good grades and receiving my parents' praise... but that kind of satisfaction wore thin quickly.
But if the things I'd done and learned could be connected to someone else's happiness—rather than serving as petty self-satisfaction—how wonderful, how gratifying that would be.
——And I felt, strongly, that I could come to like the version of myself who thought that way.
``Kei-chan...``
Mion listened to all of this with a gentle expression, never once interrupting. Not a single teasing remark left her lips, which was so unlike her.
I stopped myself there, then continued as though I'd just remembered something.

``...In the end, I plan to come back to Hinamizawa.@k@vS14/01/210101247. This is the place that helped me find myself again.@k@vS14/01/210101248. If I didn't repay that debt, I'd deserve whatever divine punishment came my way.``
``Ahahahahaha!@k@vS14/03/210300641. No doubt about that~!``
Mion beamed with delight, answering me with the brightest smile I'd ever seen. And then—
changeReason: Narrative perspective shifts from third person to first person.

TextENGNew: ``Yep... that's so like you, Kei-chan.``
``I'm so glad you're you, Kei-chan. I really am.@k@vS14/03/210300644. ...Thank you.``
A warmth I'd never felt before filled the space between us.

Going to college in Tokyo together... then coming back to Hinamizawa together... The dream swelled, vast and boundless. It felt as though an endlessly bright future lay open before us, and a quiet contentment washed over me.
Just then.
An unexpected voice from behind scattered that warm atmosphere like mist.
``Wait... Shion?``
I turned around, and sure enough, there stood Shion.
And right beside her——!
``My my, hello there.@k@vS14/09/210900025. Keiichi-kun, Mion-chan.``
I struggled to compose myself and return the greeting. The person with Shion was... Miyo Takano-san.
This woman... is our enemy...?!

A bone-deep tremor ran through me, as though every drop of blood in my body had frozen solid, and I barely stopped myself from tensing up visibly. Despite the air conditioning, cold sweat erupted across my back in a slick film.
...No—don't give anything away. I don't know anything. This woman is just an ordinary nurse at the Irie Clinic...!
I fought desperately to steady my ragged breathing and suppress the pounding of my heart, forcing a smile onto my face.
...I wasn't sure how convincing it was, but Takano-san simply smiled pleasantly, glancing between me and Mion as she spoke.
``Tee hee hee...@k@vS14/09/210900027. Are you two on a date?``
``Oh, no, um...``
The word 'date' sent Mion into a fluster. ...Thankfully, it seemed Takano attributed my stiff, jerky behavior to simple nervousness. ...Good.
Watching us with clear amusement, Shion flashed me a knowing grin.
changeReason: New translation is entirely different content from the original, indicating a misaligned or completely different passage.

TextENGNew: ``Hehehe... What's this, Kei-chan?@k@vS14/06/210600410. A date at the library? Not exactly dripping with romance~``
``I-it's perfectly fine.@k@vS14/01/210101255. The library is cool, quiet, comfortable—great place to spend time, right~?``
``And it's free, too.@k@vS14/06/210600412. You're surprisingly stingy, Kei-chan.@k@vS14/06/210600413. You know, the depth of one's love is measured by how much money one spends~``
What was she, some office lady? And 'how much money'—I was a student with no job. There was only so much I could spend.
...Okay, I was mostly back to normal now.
``Well then, I should be going.``
``Oh, right.@k@vS14/06/210600415. Thank you very much.``
``You two have fun now.``
As she turned to leave, Takano-san handed something—a file of some sort—to Shion, then left Mion and me with a meaningful smile before walking away.
Was she teasing us? What was that smile about...?
What a surprising pair, though. ...Shion and Takano-san. What connection could there possibly be between those two?
And what were those files Takano-san had given Shion? ...I couldn't explain it, but a deep unease stirred in my gut—though perhaps that was just the wariness I felt toward her.
``Hey, Shion.@k@vS14/01/210101257. What are those...?``
I pointed to the files in Shion's hands.

``Oh, these?@k@vS14/06/210600417. Takano-san compiled notes on various stories about Hinamizawa.@k@vS14/06/210600418. Things like the legend of being 'demoned away,' and other local lore... all sorts of things.``
``Being demoned away... you mean, like at Watanagashi?``

``Oh? You know about that, Kei-chan?@k@vS14/06/210600420. Takano-san's been researching the curse that happens every year on the night of Watanagashi.@k@vS14/06/210600421. ...Are you interested, Kei-chan?``
``Well, I wouldn't say interested exactly, it's more like...``
``——W-w-wait a minute, Kei-chan!``
The moment she heard that, Mion barged in—practically cutting off Shion mid-sentence. The sheer ferocity of her interruption nearly knocked me off balance.
``Wh-what's the matter, Mion?``
``I-it's nothing...``
``N-never mind that, we came here to study today, remember?!@k@vS14/03/210300647. You promised to teach me, so take it seriously~!``
``Oh, sorry... it'll just be a moment.@k@vS14/01/210101262. So, Shion... what exactly is in those files?``
``Well, I just received them, so...@k@vS14/06/210600423. Would you like to look through them together?``
``——Kei-chan, come on!``
Mion seized my arm. Her eyes were narrowed and she was clearly furious.
...Right—today was supposed to be our study date, just the two of us. No, our 'date' in the guise of a study session. Being ignored in that situation would make anyone angry.
There was nothing for it. ...Takano-san's notes nagged at me, but I decided to drop the subject with Shion.
``Sorry.@k@vS14/01/210101264. That was wrong of me... Mion.``
``Y-yeah...@k@vS14/03/210300650. So, about this problem...``
``Sorry for holding you up too, Shion.@k@vS14/01/210101266. Anyway, we need to get back to studying.``
``Hmm...@k@vS14/06/210600425. Same as always, aren't you, Sis?@k@vS14/06/210600426. Hehehe...``
Shion's eyes gleamed with a faintly condescending mirth as she snickered at Mion. Stung by it, Mion shot back a sharp glare, her expression sour.
``Wh... what's that supposed to mean?``
``You don't want your darling Kei-chan hearing about the dark side of Hinamizawa, is that it?@k@vS14/06/210600428. ...How very noble of you.``
``...Sh-Shion! You—!``
``Oh? Did I touch a nerve?@k@vS14/06/210600430. The future family head is looking rather rattled.``
*CRASH*
Mion kicked her chair aside and shot to her feet, bearing down on Shion with undisguised fury. Shion met her head-on, matching her glare for glare.
``You still don't trust me?@k@vS14/03/210300654. Keep pushing me and I won't be responsible for what happens!``

``And what might that be...?@k@vS14/06/210600432. Is that a threat from the illustrious future head?@k@vS14/06/210600433. Let me guess—I'm the one who'll be spirited away at this year's Watanagashi?``
``Don't be ridiculous!@k@vS14/03/210300656. I don't know who put those ideas in your head, but how dare you spout such nonsense!!``
``Nonsense?``
``...Then tell me the truth!@k@vS14/06/210600436. All I want is to know the truth! What's wrong with trying to find it?!``
``Th-that's... well...!``
Pressed by the force of Shion's demand, Mion suddenly faltered and stumbled over her words. That only seemed to fan Shion's rage further—she closed in on Mion, her fury laid bare.
``You're always like this, Sis!@k@vS14/06/210600438. You call yourself the next head, but all you do is read the room and dodge the issues, slithering around and never committing!``

``But I'm not like you!@k@vS14/06/210600440. If nobody wants to talk about Satoshi-kun, then fine—I'll investigate on my own!@k@vS14/06/210600441. I don't need your help, so just stay out of my way!!``
``Th... that's enough, you absolute idiot!!``
When I saw Mion raise her fist in a blind rage, I grabbed her from behind and held her back. I turned her to face me, then looked over her shoulder at Shion.
``Sorry for keeping you, Shion.@k@vS14/01/210101269. ...That story about the demoning away—I'd like to hear it sometime when we get the chance.``
``...Hmph.@k@vS14/06/210600443. The two of you can study or do whatever you want on your own!@k@vS14/06/210600444. You baby couple!!``
Shion spat those words with obvious displeasure and strode away. Watching her go, I guided Mion back into her seat.
``I owe you an apology too, Mion.@k@vS14/01/210101271. I'm sorry...``
``It's fine.@k@vS14/03/210300662. You don't have anything to apologize for...``
An uncomfortable silence hung in the air. ...Hoping to break through it, I brought up the subject of their argument.
``Hey, Mion.@k@vS14/01/210101273. ...That stuff Shion said about Satoshi—what was that about?``
``What, Kei-chan...? You want to know?``
Mion grimaced with obvious reluctance. ...Still, it wasn't mere curiosity driving me. I wanted to hear it properly, to verify what Rika-chan had told me.
``Mion... you obviously know, right?@k@vS14/01/210101275. ...About the demoning away.``
``Y-yeah... more or less...``
She was being unusually evasive. ...Most likely for the reasons Akane-san had mentioned to me.
``...Look, I'm not trying to corner you, okay?@k@vS14/01/VT4Bc_keiichi1002b. If you don't want to talk about it, I won't force you.``
``It's not a pleasant story, you know...?``
Mion lowered her gaze uneasily, then raised her head just slightly.
...I could tell her hesitation came from a desire to shield me from an unpleasant truth—a kindness—so I smiled to set her at ease.

``Pleasant or unpleasant, disturbing or anything else, I don't care.@k@vS14/01/210101278. And hearing it won't change how I feel about Hinamizawa, or about you and everyone else.@k@vS14/01/210101279. I made a promise to Akane-san—to your mother.``
``Mom said that...?@k@vS14/03/210300667. So, Kei-chan, you already...?``
I nodded, then told her about the things Akane-san had shared with me the other night—about Hinamizawa's past, and about Mion and the others.
...Naturally, Mion frowned at first upon learning that the things she'd kept hidden were already known to me.
But when I assured her that my feelings and my trust hadn't changed one bit, she let out a bittersweet smile of relief and seemed to accept it.
And then...
``...All right. Then I'll tell you what I know...``
With that preamble, Mion slowly began to speak.
...It was the story of the recurring curse in Hinamizawa. ...Though most of it overlapped with what I'd already heard from Akane-san and Rika-chan.

For the past four years, one person had died and one had vanished on the day of the Watanagashi festival, every single year. And the people of Hinamizawa secretly whispered that these were the workings of their guardian deity, Oyashiro-sama.

Being 'demoned away' was akin to being spirited away... it referred to someone vanishing as a result of Oyashiro-sama's curse. Hearing it through Mion—someone at the very heart of village life—gave me a far more vivid sense of the awe and dread the villagers felt.
...So that meant the files Takano-san gave Shion contained various theories and analyses related to the demoning away phenomenon.
It was surprising enough that Takano was researching such things, but I was even more taken aback to learn how fixated Shion was on the topic.
``...Why is Shion so desperate to investigate this?``
``...Satoko has an older brother named Satoshi.@k@vS14/03/210300670. Satoshi and Shion were very close, so...``
``...Satoshi?``
``Shion was... in love with Satoshi.``
``...Last year, Satoko's aunt was murdered and her brother disappeared.``
I remembered—Rika-chan had told me about that before.
...I see. So Shion had been in love with Satoshi. That must be why she cared so deeply about his little sister, Satoko.
``But... Satoshi vanished.@k@vS14/03/210300673. Last year, on the day of the Watanagashi festival.``
``And that was... the demoning away...?``
Mion nodded. ...So that was it. Shion was investigating the demoning away phenomenon because she wanted to find out what happened to Satoshi.
changeReason: Narrative perspective shifts from first person to third person.

TextENGNew: ``Honestly, I think she's being reckless.@k@vS14/03/210300675. Shion seems to suspect the Sonozaki family is behind the people being demoned away.@k@vS14/03/210300676. She's been poking around everywhere, digging into anything connected to the Sonozakis...``
``After everything I've done to help her, she still doesn't understand how I feel...!``
``...But isn't that... perfectly normal?``
Mion looked up at me in surprise, as if that was the last thing she expected to hear. ...I wasn't trying to take Shion's side, but I spoke what I honestly felt.
``What if... hypothetically, you were the one who disappeared, Mion?@k@vS14/01/210101285. I'm sure I'd be doing the exact same thing Shion is.@k@vS14/01/210101286. Wouldn't you, too?``
``W-well, of course I would!@k@vS14/03/210300680. I'd do everything in my power to find you, Kei-chan!@k@vS14/03/210300681. And I'd— ...Ah...!``
Mion caught herself mid-sentence, stared at me, then bit her lip and hung her head.
...Then, as though something was coming back to her, she clutched her head with a pale expression, murmuring something I couldn't quite make out——

``Shion, you've committed a great sin.@k@vS14/06/150602133. You were Mion until now.@k@vS14/06/150602134. You had the powerful position of Mion, heir to the Sonozaki demon bloodline, and yet you did nothing to save Satoshi-kun—you didn't even try!``
``You had a billion moments in which you could have saved Satoshi-kun, and you chose to let every single one of them die.``
``I'll never forgive you. I could have saved Satoshi-kun if I'd been in your place!!@k@vS14/06/150602137. You stole my chance and you killed him—I will never, ever forgive you for that!!!``
``I will kill you. I will kill you without fail!!@k@vS14/06/150602139. Starting today, I will spend every waking moment devising the most cruel and agonizing way to end your life!!``
``Shion, you can spend every fleeting day left to you trembling in fear of when and how you'll die!!!``

``I won't make it quick—I'll make it as gruesome as I can!!!@k@vS14/06/150602142. And when you're dead, I'll throw you into the same well as Satoshi-kun.@k@vS14/06/150602143. You can apologize to him in the next world!!!``
``That's what you deserve for impersonating me all this time!!@k@vS14/06/150602145. Retribution!!@k@vS14/06/150602146. Your sin!!@k@vS14/06/150602147. AAAAAAAAAAHHHHH!!!``
The demonic visage of 'Mion' was simply terrifying. All I could do was hug my knees and tremble.
...But now, I understand.
If I had been in that 'Mion's' position... what would I think? What would I do...?
``I understand... I understand now...@k@vS14/03/210300683. No... I've only just finally realized...!``

``I thought I was doing my best as Satoshi's friend.@k@vS14/03/210300685. But for Shion... it wasn't the same. It was something far beyond that.@k@vS14/03/210300686. I never truly understood how she felt...``
I recalled the exchange between Mion and Shion moments ago.
Shion's reaction may have been excessive... but when I thought about it calmly, I could glimpse the feelings buried beneath it.
...Shion still carried Satoshi in her heart, even now—Satoshi, who had been demoned away and vanished.
But she couldn't find him. ...And the more she hit dead ends, the more she was consumed by anger with no outlet, by sadness, by anxiety—and her heart grew more ravaged with each passing day.
That pain swelled without limit, eventually fermenting into suspicion and dark emotion——
``...Hmph.@k@vS14/06/210600446. You two enjoy studying or whatever on your own!@k@vS14/06/210600447. Have fun on your little baby date!!``
I couldn't begin to imagine how Shion must have felt, seeing the two of us together while carrying all of that inside her.
...In a scene she'd normally shrug off, her raw nerves drove her to lash out with words she didn't mean.
And then, loathing herself for feeling that way, her emotions grew even more turbulent, more jagged—and who could blame her for that?
``I have... you, Kei-chan.@k@vS14/03/210300688. But Shion... doesn't have Satoshi anymore...``
``You're right... the timing couldn't have been worse.``
``...No, it's not just the timing.@k@vS14/03/210300690. I thought I understood how Shion felt... but I didn't understand her at all.@k@vS14/03/210300691. Of course she'd be upset...``
``When I think about it from Shion's perspective... I never did anything for her at all...``
Mion hung her head. Her long hair fell forward like a curtain, hiding her anguished face.
``How pathetic of me...@k@vS14/03/210300694. To only realize something so obvious now, in this way...@k@vS14/03/210300695. I'm really... such a...``
The person before me was neither the head of the Sonozaki family nor the leader of our club... She was simply a girl. And above all else—an older sister, thinking of her younger sister.
And so.
There was never any question about what words to offer her.
``——'I'm sorry,' right?``
``Then go apologize.@k@vS14/01/210101292. It's not like she's someone you can never see again. If you feel bad, just say so.@k@vS14/01/210101293. Go tell Shion you're sorry.``
``Kei-chan... but...``
Mion cast her eyes down, looking pained. Then she spoke in barely more than a whisper.

``After what just happened... she might not even agree to see me.@k@vS14/03/210300699. Even if I call, she might not pick up...@k@vS14/03/210300700. And even if I apologize for everything... she might not forgive me—``
``Maybe so.@k@vS14/01/210101295. ——But the only one who can find out for certain is you, Mion.``
Yes. No one else could do it for her—only Mion could take that step. The answer would come only if she acted on her own...

``You'll never know unless you try.@k@vS14/01/210101297. And if you feel that strongly about it, don't hold back—throw everything you've got at it.@k@vS14/01/210101298. ...And if it doesn't work out, I'll be here to pick you back up.``
``Kei-chan...``
Mion sniffled and wiped the tears that had welled at the corners of her eyes. Then she took one deep breath to steady herself and nodded firmly.
``...You're right, yeah...@k@vS14/03/210300704. I understand.@k@vS14/03/210300705. Tonight, I'll go to her apartment.``
``Good.``

``If it doesn't work, I'll call you.@k@vS14/03/210300707. ...No, actually, never mind that.@k@vS14/03/210300708. I'm not even going to consider failure.@k@vS14/03/210300709. I'll make this work, no matter what... so cheer me on, okay?``
``Of course.@k@vS14/01/210101301. You're my precious friend, Mion.``
``——Thanks, Kei-chan.``
Mion said that softly as she held her hand out to me. I squeezed it with all my strength without a word. Even though it must have hurt, she turned to me with the happiest face I'd seen.
You can do this, Mion. I know it'll work out.
So.
Shion—I hope you'll try to understand, too.
All I could do now was believe in the two of them.
